events: Death King takes Raviel and Uburka to fast food restaurant in side story world
Raviel and Uburka have friendly conversation ignoring Death King
Death King notices pedestrian with strange steady gait wearing Shinseo Middle-High uniform
Pedestrian has blonde hair, carries old briefcase, expressionless face
Death King follows pedestrian and recognizes Tower Master's voice
Reveals Tower Master is living as middle school student in this world
cause_effect: The side story world uses data from Death King's memories but Kim Yul's face came from Tower Master's memories
The pedestrian's absolute regularity of movement reveals divine nature hidden in human form
Death King's recognition of Tower Master confirms the Lady's words: Towers are built by Masters living the unhappiest lives
notes: The world-wide perception alteration makes Raviel's empire dress and Uburka's hobgoblin appearance seem normal to locals. Tower Master's walking rhythm is described as absolutely unchanging - like a being from another time. Death King recalls the Lady's explanation: The Master lives on behalf of the unhappiest person in the world.

events: Death King follows Tower Master to dilapidated hanok village
Ja Soo-jung introduces herself as 3rd year Shinseo Middle School student
Reveals her uncle was killed by robbers, lives alone
Claims to know nothing about Tower, Pillars, or Hunters
Death King realizes she has no memories of being Tower Master
Ja Soo-jung demonstrates extreme observational intelligence analyzing Death King's behavior
Shows warehouse with possessed objects: mirror that shows loved ones, puppy doll named Meow that makes cat sounds
Reveals she can see Bae Hu-ryeong - first person besides Death King to do so
cause_effect: Ja Soo-jung's amnesia is self-imposed restriction - she lives as ordinary person unless urgent situation arises
Her ability to see ghosts and cursed objects hints at her true divine nature
The possessed items are dangerous but she contains them to protect others
notes: Ja Soo-jung's name is written as 慈水晶 (Amethyst). She demonstrates extreme logical reasoning to determine Death King is not a criminal. The warehouse contains multiple cursed items she cares for like children. She mentions 'kids play at 1 am' - referring to the ghosts/entities.

characters: The Lady Who Walks Through the Mirage (mentioned)
events: Raviel researches Ja Soo-jung using newspapers - discovers she's known as 'Ghost Seeing Detective'
Media exposes real name of minor, 12 administrative measures, 8 civil suits against her
Death King explains contradiction in trauma manifestation: Constellation Killer cannot remember Kim Yul
Tower extracted data from Death King's memories for faces but needed Tower Master's memory for Kim Yul's face
Lord of Manseng accepted stage clear instead of Head Librarian Hamustra - proving Tower Master managed this stage
Death King deduces he can trigger Tower Master's appearance by creating another contradiction: dying at Ja Soo-jung's hands
Raviel allows it on condition he dies in front of her
cause_effect: The Constellation Killer's lost memory created a contradiction that required Tower Master intervention
Death King realizes this is his only chance to learn Tower Master's secrets before higher floors
Bae Hu-ryeong confirms no Tower Master information existed up to 99th floor
notes: Death King explains two types of trauma: what killer remembers vs what profoundly impacted their life regardless of memory. Kim Yul's face appearing correctly proves Tower Master provided the data. The Lady Who Walks Through the Mirage is revealed to be Tower Master's daughter.

events: Death King tests Ja Soo-jung's compliance with unreasonable requests: sit on table, put hand on his head
Ja Soo-jung complies with every request without hesitation
Raviel confirms Ja Soo-jung never refuses any sincere request
Death King shows Returner's Clockwork Watch as proof of other worlds
Tells his life story for 1.5 hours
Ja Soo-jung agrees to kill him once
She requests he tell Tower Master she's happy and loves the city and children
Ja Soo-jung stabs Death King in throat with dagger
deaths: Kim Gongja (stabbed in throat by Ja Soo-jung)
loops: Death by Ja Soo-jung's hand triggers trauma manifestation
cause_effect: Ja Soo-jung's absolute compliance reveals she sees everyone as children to care for, not an AI
Her final words 'I'm happy' show she genuinely loves her life despite the hardship
Death King's death creates the contradiction needed to summon Tower Master
notes: Ja Soo-jung's compliance is absolute - she would follow a stranger into captivity or worse if asked sincerely. She sells Raviel's emerald through classmate Lee Mi-ho whose family is in secondary finance/construction (implied gangster connections). Death King describes her as a 'mommy' figure.

characters: Lord of Manseng/Tower Master (Ja Soo-jung awakened form)
The Lady Who Walks Through the Mirage (mentioned)
events: Death King's trauma manifestation fails - data rejected
Tower system requests approval from Zrakua (Tower Master's name in system)
Approval denied - issue not on agenda
Lord of Manseng manifests directly, grabs Death King's chin
Tower Master introduces herself: Lord of Manseng, Ja Soo-jung (紫水晶/Amethyst), Man Hwa-gyeong (萬華鏡/Kaleidoscope)
Reveals Returner's Clockwork Watch's trauma penalty was originally her ancestor's ability called Manseng (萬生)
Reveals The Lady Who Walks Through the Mirage is her DAUGHTER
Tower Master offers to remove trauma penalty and give better skill if Death King gives up
Death King refuses
Tower Master blesses him and activates Animal Road trauma level
Trauma shows Ja Soo-jung's birth as Baron Amethyst's daughter in dying kingdom
skills: Blessing of the Lord of Manseng
cause_effect: Tower Master's direct intervention confirms she manages this stage personally
The trauma penalty skill originated from Tower Master's ancestor (Dragon Emperor)
Her daughter created Returner's Clockwork Watch based on mother's ability
Tower Master's rough personality contrasts sharply with amnesiac Ja Soo-jung's emotionlessness
notes: Tower Master has peach scent. She repeatedly says 'shit' and threatens to turn Death King's nostrils inside out. She reveals her daughter (The Lady) is a serious mama's girl who carries a peach-patterned pillow. The six roads of Manseng are revealed: Hell Road, Hungry Ghost Road, Animal Road, Sura Road, Human Road, Heaven Road.

events: Kingdom on brink of collapse, conflict between humans and sub races
Conservative faction proposes 7th Unified Marriage Act to restrict mixing
Baron Citrine declares family war on Amethyst family
Ja Soo-jung defeats Citrine through fabricated reports and hidden Archmage vassal
Ja Soo-jung beheads enemy vassals one by one in front of Baron Citrine
Forces Baron Citrine to marry her (same-sex marriage) as condition for sparing remaining vassals
Political middle finger to conservative faction
Skill revealed: [Madness to Insanity] - curse from Dragon preventing loss of mind
deaths: Baron Citrine's vassals (beheaded by Ja Soo-jung)
Ja Soo-jung's father (implied, killed by her)
cause_effect: Ja Soo-jung's marriage to Baron Citrine merges divided families after 6 generations
Her 'madness' is actually absolute clarity - she cannot lose her mind due to Dragon's curse
The conservative faction's attack backfires completely
notes: Ja Soo-jung and Hwang Soo-jung were childhood friends, even kidnapped together by terrorists. Ja Soo-jung's comment 'This one wanted to do this' reveals her proactive cruelty. The marriage is officiated in court with conservative nobles present.

events: Ja Soo-jung gains favor of Apathetic Duke - one of six great nobles
Witch Hunt begins: targets Black Witch terrorist organization
Ja Soo-jung seduces Princess Cham Beol-sa using psychological manipulation
Skill: [King's Tutor] - instinctively spots enemy weaknesses
Ja Soo-jung captures Red Nail rebels, guarantees safety to those who surrender
Converts former terrorists into her vassals/soldiers/citizens
Skill: [Guardian of the Subrace] - easier to gain sub race trust but humans despise her
Uses Crown Princess as bait to trap Black Witch
Black Witch reveals they don't want compensation - they want humans to feel pain
Black Witch dies cursing Ja Soo-jung
Ja Soo-jung realizes she cannot help the dead, decides to find Dragons who might know how to treat death
deaths: Countless humans killed by Red Nails
cause_effect: Ja Soo-jung's sub race protection creates Mirror Faction of outcasts and criminals
The Black Witch's curse makes Ja Soo-jung confront her own incompetence regarding death
Her decision to hunt Dragons sets up Act 2
notes: Princess Cham Beol-sa's hobby is cutting off criminals' right arms. Ja Soo-jung acts as sister figure to dissolve princess's childhood trauma. The Black Witch's final words reveal the rebels' pain is about already-dead children who can never return.

events: Gold Dragon lives in Golden Sky Tower/Netherworld Tower for 7,000 years
Kingdom's 1st, 2nd, 3rd subjugation orders all failed due to Gold Dragon's [Instinct] power
Ja Soo-jung approaches Gold Dragon with warriors who love her more than their own greed
Gold Dragon's power liberates human greed but Ja Soo-jung's followers fear disappointing her more than their desires
Gold Dragon realizes they are dominated by love/fear of Ja Soo-jung
Gold Dragon falls in love and surrenders
Reveals Earth Bone Dragon's power: archives memories in 'box', grants immortality
Reveals Ice River Dragon's power: freezes people in ice pillars, stops time
Gold Dragon becomes Ja Soo-jung's pet
Earth Bone Dragon and Ice River Dragon also captured
skills: Gold Dragon's Gaze (EX) - liberates instinct, reveals essence
Earth Bone Dragon's Skull (SSS+) - memory archiving box
Ice River Dragon's Breath (EX) - time control
cause_effect: The three Dragon Emperors' powers form foundation of future Tower: Eyes (observation), Skull (memory storage), Breath (time control)
Love is revealed as the strategy that defeated the 'impregnable' Netherworld Tower
Gold Dragon's surrender proves Ja Soo-jung's charisma transcends racial/species boundaries
notes: The Earth Bone Dragon's Box can recreate bodies with same memories repeatedly. The Ice River Dragon can send people back to past or freeze them, but only to times the Dragon observed. Ja Soo-jung now has three Dragon 'pets' who love her.

characters: Kim Gongja/Death King (as Baron Gu Won-ha)
events: Death King wakes up possessing Baron Gu Won-ha's body in Tower Master's trauma
Experiences memory instability and ego weakening
Uses magic spells to restore information about surrounding nobles
Discovers Baek Seol-to killed a freeman child with whip at age 12
Discovers Sun Heuk-sin was former cannibal who ate 300+ humans in Ritual of Darkness
Both are Ja Soo-jung's lovers/allies
Viscount Ja Soo-jung sits under throne with King stroking her head
Death King's ego wavers between Gu Won-ha's personality and his own
cause_effect: Death King's possession of Gu Won-ha allows him to observe Tower Master's past from inside
The trauma actively tries to suppress his ego and make him believe he's Gu Won-ha
Information spells reveal all of Ja Soo-jung's lovers are murderers/criminals
notes: Gu Won-ha is a 2nd Stage Ascendant Wizard - one of only twelve in the world. Her body is literally made of mana/water. Ja Soo-jung's faction is called 'Mirror Faction.' The Platinum Tower has water channels with 'amniotic fluid' flowing beneath seats.

characters: Kim Gongja/Death King (as Baron Gu Won-ha)
events: Death King realizes the Tower is a gigantic memory box using Earth Bone Dragon's power
Deduces that everyone who enters has memories archived in the Tower
This explains how Returner's Clockwork Watch can restore 4,000 days - data restoration points
Tower confirms first correct answer: Earth Bone Dragon's Box forms outer shell
Death King struggles with second gateway: how worlds are brought into Tower
Viscount Ja Soo-jung detects his changed consciousness and asks what he's doing
Death King reveals he's from future where Tower already exists
Ja Soo-jung realizes he's not Gu Won-ha
Death King reveals his real name and that he's a Hunter from Ascension City/Spire World
Ja Soo-jung accepts this and offers to show him her world
cause_effect: Death King becomes first to discover Tower's structural foundation
The Earth Bone Dragon's Box stores all hunters' memories and allows body recreation
Viscount Ja Soo-jung's awareness within her own trauma shows she has some meta-cognition even in past form
The Tower's naming (Spire World) seems intentional to Ja Soo-jung
notes: The Tower congratulates Death King for being first to discover structure. Gu Won-ha's body has fire core (1st Advisor) and water core (2nd Advisor - Gu Won-ha herself). Ja Soo-jung calls her water 'personal lip balm, washroom, and sink.' Death King realizes he's possessing a FEMALE body - Baron Gu Won-ha is a woman.

characters: Kim Gongja/Death King (as Baron Gu Won-ha)
events: Ja Soo-jung explains 2nd Stage Ascendants - closer to divine beings, can escape human limits
Gu Won-ha's core is water - extremely clean and cool
Ja Soo-jung uses Gu Won-ha's water as personal lip balm/washroom
They enter lounge where Mirror Faction nobles gather
Death King examines each noble's information using Gu Won-ha's spells
Reveals ALL of Ja Soo-jung's lovers/followers are murderers, cannibals, arsonists, poisoners, butchers
Dae Ha-ran: former general who killed 210,000 including civilians
Hae Meong-sum: former assassin guild master, arsonist, poisoner
Gu Se-ju: rebel right hand who tortured/killed hundreds
Se Ah-gwi: ate thousands of people in Ritual of Darkness
Death King realizes common thread: Ja Soo-jung surrounds herself exclusively with sinners
cause_effect: Every person in Ja Soo-jung's faction has committed atrocities but serves her loyally
Death King begins to understand Ja Soo-jung's true nature through her choice of companions
The Mirror Faction is literally a collection of the kingdom's worst criminals
notes: Gu Won-ha's family history: uncle, father, and adopted daughter all participated in different rebellions. Ja Soo-jung can speak German, English, French, Korean, and Japanese from foreign tutors. She mentions outsiders from other worlds who came to her world but were imprisoned or killed.

characters: Kim Gongja/Death King (as Baron Gu Won-ha)
events: Death King asks why Ja Soo-jung only gathers murderers as lovers
Ja Soo-jung asks if he thinks executing sinners is right
She demonstrates Count Ah Ru-ho's hypocrisy - he also has blood on his hands
Ja Soo-jung reveals her philosophy: pain is the only answer
The law should return exact same pain to sinners that victims suffered
She plans to refound kingdom under 'Law of Pain'
The Tower is a tool to accomplish this
She declares gaining power is the only way to make impossible possible
She has seduced six Dukes, King, and captured three Dragon Emperors to become most powerful
Taps chair twice - entire lounge falls silent instantly
She will show Death King a 'miracle' tonight
cause_effect: Ja Soo-jung's Law of Pain reveals her motivation for building Tower: to create system where all suffering can be precisely returned to wrongdoers
Her 'miracle' is the council meeting where she will implement this law
Death King realizes she's completely different from him - he hesitates at executing sinners, she embraces it as necessity
notes: Ja Soo-jung says there are two ways to make impossible possible: go crazy, or gain power. The entire lounge - hundreds of powerful nobles - goes completely silent at her two taps, showing absolute control.

characters: Kim Gongja/Death King (as Baron Gu Won-ha)
events: Council meeting resumes in circular hall with amniotic fluid channels
Count Ah Ru-ho accuses Ja Soo-jung of harboring rebels and sub race terrorists
Ja Soo-jung defends them as her captured property/belongings
Count brings witness: old man Nam Suk whose granddaughter was killed by Marquis Baek Seol-to
The hairpin the granddaughter was carrying is presented as evidence
Marquis Baek Seol-to confesses she killed the child 6 years ago
Ja Soo-jung admits she knew and accepted Marquis anyway
Ja Soo-jung reveals Marquis's abusive childhood under genius grandfather
Explains she 'raised' Marquis properly by showing her real world suffering
Count calls Ja Soo-jung rotten; she says the point is Marquis will be unhappier the better she becomes
deaths: Nam Suk's granddaughter (whipped to death by Young Miss Baek Seol-to, 6 years ago)
cause_effect: Ja Soo-jung deliberately raises Marquis Baek Seol-to be great noble while ensuring her past sin makes her perpetually unhappy
She considers this 'lovely' - the combination of competence and unhappiness
The council meeting is revealed to be proceeding exactly as Ja Soo-jung planned
notes: The amniotic fluid changes color based on agenda importance - would turn red for war. The granddaughter's name was Ari. Baek Seol-to was 10 years old when she killed the child. The brooch she made for her grandfather was stepped on and broken by the commoner girl, triggering the rage.

characters: Kim Gongja/Death King (as Baron Gu Won-ha)
events: Death King deduces Ja Soo-jung has Gold Dragon's Eyes AND Ice River Dragon's Breath
Gold Dragon's Eyes allow omniscient viewpoint - can see anything
Ice River Dragon's Breath allows traveling to any observed time
Combination = she personally witnessed the granddaughter's death
Tower confirms second correct answer: Gold Dragon's Eyes and Ice River Dragon's Breath form Tower's Observatory
Ja Soo-jung takes Death King back in time to witness the whipping death personally
She reveals she has lived infinite lives within 18 years, watched countless deaths
She could prevent any death by regressing but chooses NEVER to interfere
By not interfering, ALL misfortune becomes HER fault - the only God who can be condemned
She shows other sinners' crimes: Sun Heuk-sin eating human flesh, Dae Ha-ran burning villages
locations: Past - snowy street where Ari died
Cave where Sun Heuk-sin ate humans
skills: Ice River Dragon's Breath (time travel to observed moments)
deaths: Village civilians burned by Dae Ha-ran
cause_effect: Ja Soo-jung's refusal to interfere makes her the 'God that never Regresses' - she accepts all blame to give victims someone to condemn
Her omniscience + time travel = she witnesses every tragedy in her kingdom personally
She tells Death King to blame her, scream at her - it won't be meaningless because she COULD have helped
notes: The death scene is graphic: 10-year-old Baek Seol-to whipping a child to death over a broken brooch. Ja Soo-jung reveals she watched 'billions, trillions' cross the yellow river. She is 'A God who would not help anyone, even if she regressed.'

characters: Kim Gongja/Death King (as Baron Gu Won-ha)
events: Ja Soo-jung accepts full blame: 'This one is the greatest sinner in kingdom history'
Proposes bill called 'The Golden Rule'
Reveals it was pre-debated among the Six Dukes - Ten Thousand God Duke was in on it
The Golden Rule: Seventh Duke (Ja Soo-jung) accepts all responsibility for kingdom's sins
Any subject ruined by kingdom can inflict exact same suffering on Ja Soo-jung
Victims can force the actual culprit to attend as witness
Culprit must support Ja Soo-jung during punishment, smell her blood from closest distance
Annual event: every December 24th, midnight, Ja Soo-jung circles Royal Capital clockwise
Starting/ending at Platinum Square
Any subject of any age/sex can demand Golden Rule execution
Ja Soo-jung uses Gold Dragon's Eyes to measure suffering and Ice River Dragon's Breath to verify past
skills: Ice River Dragon's Breath (verify past suffering)
cause_effect: The Golden Rule makes Ja Soo-jung the kingdom's scapegoat - she absorbs all punishment
Her lovers who committed sins must watch her suffer their consequences
The bill transforms her from villain to sacrificial God figure
Count Ah Ru-ho realizes too late the entire proceeding was Ja Soo-jung's plan
notes: Today is December 24th - the first Golden Rule night. The Ten Thousand God Duke apologizes to Count for keeping secret: 'Big sis asked me to.' Ja Soo-jung's faction nobles all lower their heads when the bill is read - they knew. The Country Tilting Duke (傾國公) is her shortened title.
